gmi_articles.title: A phyto-compund significantly improves gastric emptying in dyspeptic patients.
A standardized extract of ginger and artichoke significantly promoted gastric emptying in healthy volunteers.
Acupuncture in critically ill patients improves delayed gastric emptying.
Acupuncture is more effective than the drug Motilium in treating gastroparesis.
CBD provides symptom relief in patients with gastroparesis and improves the tolerance of liquid nutrient intake.
Electroacupuncture effectively reduces the dyspeptic symptoms of diabetic gastroparesis and accelerates solid gastric emptying.
Ginger extract reduces delayed gastric emptying and nosocomial pneumonia in adult respiratory distress syndrome patients hospitalized in an intensive care unit.
Lactobacillus reuteri accelerates gastric emptying and improves regurgitation in infants.
Peppermint may have therapeutic value in gastric emptying disorders.
The herbal preparation known as Iberogast is as efficacious as cisapride for the treatment of patients with functional dyspepsia of dismotility type.
Pulmonary aspiration of gastric contents in two patients taking semaglutide for weight loss.
Tendency of semaglutide to induce gastroparesis: A case report.
Electroacupuncture at ST36 protects ICC networks via mSCF/Kit-ETV1 signalling in the stomach of diabetic mice.
Glutamine could act as a motility-recovery agent after gastrectomy in humans.
Glutamine decreases the duration of postoperative ileus after abdominal surgery.
Orthosiphon stamineus leaf extract protects against ethanol-induced gastropathy in rats.
The essential oil from Atractylodes lancea may have benefit in ameliorating delayed gastric emptying.
Patients with diabetes or idiopathic gastroparesis could benefit from dietary modifications and nutritional counseling
Transcutaneous vagal nerve stimulation for gastrointestinal disorders.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

clinical_trials.title: Efficacy and Safety of Cannabidiol for Gastroparesis and Functional Dyspepsia

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Idiopathic gastroparesis: spontaneous resolution in ~30-40% at 5 years (NIDDK natural history study); diabetic gastroparesis: rarely remits, correlated with glycaemic control; postsurgical: highly variable
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Dietary modification + glycaemic optimisation: 50-60% improvement in symptoms; metoclopramide: 30-40% short-term symptom improvement (limited by side effects); gastric electrical stimulation (Enterra Therapy): 50-60% significant symptom improvement at 3-5 years in refractory patients; pyloric procedures (G-POEM): 75-80% short-term improvement
remission.gap_size: Large — gastric electrical stimulation and pyloromyotomy are underused; metoclopramide misuse is the dominant pharmacotherapy problem
remission.barrier_detail: Metoclopramide (the only FDA-approved drug for gastroparesis since 1979) carries a black box warning for tardive dyskinesia with >12 weeks use; yet many patients are maintained long-term; gastric electrical stimulation (GES) is underutilised and restricted to few centres; domperidone (off-label, superior safety) inaccessible in US except via FDA expanded access
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — G-POEM (gastric per-oral endoscopic pyloromyotomy) increasingly adopted; trazpiroben (Phase 3 GASS trial ongoing) and relamorelin (Phase 3 failed primary endpoint 2018) attempted; metoclopramide as sole approved drug since 1979 — one of the longest-unchanged pharmacotherapy landscapes in GI medicine
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term metoclopramide >12 weeks without alternative assessment (tardive dyskinesia risk — black box warning; yet chronic prescribing common); erythromycin IV as long-term oral agent (tolerance develops within days to weeks; useful for inpatient acute management not chronic); nasojejunal tube feeding without GES consideration in severe refractory cases
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Domperidone via FDA-compassionate use IND (superior safety profile vs metoclopramide; widely used outside US); dietary: small frequent low-fat, low-fibre meals; pyloric Botox injection (short-lived effect ~3 months; predicts G-POEM response); cannabis for nausea control in refractory cases (mixed evidence; legal-access dependent)
remission.frontier_signal: G-POEM (endoscopic pyloromyotomy) — durable data accumulating; trazpiroben (D2/D3 antagonist + 5-HT4 agonist — Phase 3 GASS); velusetrag (selective 5-HT4 agonist — Phase 2 positive for symptom improvement); transcutaneous electrical acustimulation (TEA — Phase 2 data)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
